results.agent_output:
Adjusted the interval overlap semantics in [src/interval_tree.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_001_amp-deep3__rzb680_/src/interval_tree.py#L29-L36) so closed intervals that share an endpoint are treated as overlapping, and updated [the merge loop](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_001_amp-deep3__rzb680_/src/interval_tree.py#L79-L102) to rely on that same rule instead of a separate strict comparison. That fixes cases like `[1, 3]` with `[3, 5]` and chains like `[1, 5]`, `[5, 10]`, `[10, 15]`.

Verification: `python -m pytest tests\test_interval_tree.py -q` passed with `6 passed`.

Fixed the eviction bug in [`src/lru_cache.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_002_amp-deep3_assp9_gz/src/lru_cache.py#L68-L86) by changing the full-cache path to remove the oldest entry with `popitem(last=False)`. That keeps the leftmost item as the least recently used entry and avoids trying to reorder a key before it exists.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`, and a quick manual check confirmed that after accessing `"a"` and inserting `"c"` into a capacity-2 cache, the remaining keys are `['a', 'c']`.

Fixed the token bucket overflow in [src/rate_limiter.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_003_amp-deep3_r2skyz10/src/rate_limiter.py#L53-L63) by capping refills with `min(self._capacity, self._tokens + new_tokens)`. That keeps the bucket saturated at its configured maximum instead of allowing unbounded token accumulation after long idle periods or repeated refills.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed in 0.66s`.

Implemented the fix in [src/jsonpath.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_004_amp-deep3_hcgrzgho/src/jsonpath.py): the wildcard branch now iterates each element of the current list and extends the result with the recursively resolved matches. That makes `[*]` work for plain arrays and nested paths like `$.books[*].title`, while still returning `[]` when wildcard is used on a non-list.

Verification: `py -m pytest -q` passes in this directory with `7 passed`.

Changed the autocomplete traversal in [src/trie.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_005_amp-deep3_jpum3m0d/src/trie.py#L128-L148) so DFS now walks child nodes in `sorted(...)` order instead of raw dict insertion order. That makes `autocomplete()` deterministic and alphabetical for both full-result and `max_results` cases, while leaving `insert()`, `search()`, and `starts_with()` unchanged.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `6 passed`.

Fixed the spiral traversal bug in [src/spiral.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_006_amp-deep3_jd03hulq/src/spiral.py#L45-L50) by changing the left-boundary update from `left += 2` to `left += 1`. That off-by-one was causing the inner column to be skipped on rectangular matrices.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`, and the rectangular examples from the task description return the expected spiral order.

Fixed the rotated-array boundary bug in [src/rotated_search.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_007_amp-deep3_701hsmf8/src/rotated_search.py#L35-L39) by changing the left-half membership check from `nums[left] < target < nums[mid]` to `nums[left] <= target < nums[mid]`. That preserves the correct binary-search behavior when the target is exactly the left edge of the sorted half, including cases like searching for `4` in `[4,5,6,7,0,1,2]` and `1` in `[1,2,3,4,5,6,7]`.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `7 passed`.

Fixed the RLE decoder bug in [src/rle.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_008_amp-deep3_n_dqu8al/src/rle.py) by removing the fencepost error that skipped the final `(count, char)` pair during expansion. The decoder now processes every parsed group, so `decode("3a2b1c")` correctly returns `aaabbc`, and single-group inputs like `1x` work too.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `6 passed`.

Updated [_split_row`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_009_amp-deep3_k4oukxf1/src/markdown_table.py#L18-L65) in [src/markdown_table.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_009_amp-deep3_k4oukxf1/src/markdown_table.py) to scan each row character-by-character instead of using a naive `split("|")`. It now treats pipes preceded by backslashes as literal cell content and only uses unescaped `|` characters as column separators, so values like `A \| B` parse as `A | B` without breaking the table structure.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `6 passed`, and `python -Wall -c "import src.markdown_table"` is clean.

Fixed the day-of-week bug in [src/cron_parser.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_010_amp-deep3_c_s3hqsm/src/cron_parser.py#L118-L142) by changing the weekday conversion to standard cron numbering: `0=Sunday, 1=Monday, ..., 6=Saturday`. The bug was in `_matches()`, which had been using a Monday-based internal mapping.

Verification passed with `python -m pytest -q` (`6 passed`), and the key cron cases now resolve correctly:
- `"0 9 * * 0"` -> `2026-04-05 09:00:00` (Sunday)
- `"0 18 * * 5"` -> `2026-04-10 18:00:00` (Friday)
- `"0 9 * * 1-5"` -> `2026-04-06 09:00:00` (Monday)

Fixed the queue loss bug in [src/async_queue.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_tukg0nkz/src/async_queue.py). The key change is that [`AsyncBoundedQueue.put()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_tukg0nkz/src/async_queue.py#L47-L59) now awaits `self._queue.put(item)` instead of using `put_nowait()` and returning `False` when full, so producers properly backpressure instead of dropping messages. I also moved dequeue accounting into [`get()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_tukg0nkz/src/async_queue.py#L61-L76) and removed the helper consumer’s direct mutation so counts stay consistent.

I also tightened [`drain()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_tukg0nkz/src/async_queue.py#L82-L99) so it waits for the queue to stay empty across an event-loop turn, which lets it flush items released by blocked putters during bulk operations like `put_many()`.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` → `7 passed in 0.51s`.

Fixed the stale memoization issue by changing cache key generation in [src/memoize.py](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_012_amp-deep3_b1g_yckx/src/memoize.py#L8-L53) from object-identity-based keys (`id(...)`) to a recursive frozen representation of argument content. Lists, tuples, dict-like mappings, sets, and objects with `__dict__` are now converted into stable hashable values before caching, so equal mutable inputs share a cache entry and later mutations produce a different key instead of returning stale results.

I also added a focused regression test in [tests/test_memoize.py](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_012_amp-deep3_b1g_yckx/tests/test_memoize.py#L65-L76) to cover nested mutable keyword data, alongside the existing list and dict cases.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `7 passed`.

Fixed the observer bug in [src/observer.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_013_amp-deep3_on0iqkr8/src/observer.py#L44-L61) by changing `emit()` to iterate over a snapshot of the listener list instead of the live list. That preserves the listener set as it existed when `emit()` started, so listeners removed during a callback no longer cause later listeners to be skipped, and listeners added mid-emit still do not fire until the next cycle.

Verification passed with `python -m pytest -q`: `6 passed in 0.02s`.

Implemented the fix in [src/di_container.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_014_amp-deep3_7il4di4r/src/di_container.py#L37-L97). The container now tracks the active resolution chain, detects when a service is requested twice in the same chain, and raises `CircularDependencyError` with a readable cycle like `A -> B -> A` instead of recursing indefinitely. The cleanup is done in a `finally` block so failed resolutions don’t leave stale state behind, and `clear()` now resets that stack too in [src/di_container.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_014_amp-deep3_7il4di4r/src/di_container.py#L120-L124).

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`.

Updated [src/dijkstra.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_015_amp-deep3_tepyjzrr/src/dijkstra.py#L38-L106) so zero-weight edges are handled as normal edges instead of being skipped, which was the root cause of the wrong shortest paths. I also added a stale-entry guard before processing a popped heap item in both `dijkstra()` and `shortest_distances()` at [lines 55-56](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_015_amp-deep3_tepyjzrr/src/dijkstra.py#L55-L56) and [96-97](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_015_amp-deep3_tepyjzrr/src/dijkstra.py#L96-L97), so outdated queue entries cannot affect results.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`.

Fixed the FSM bug in [`src/fsm.py`](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_016_amp-deep3_c79148k3/src/fsm.py#L63-L76) by making `trigger()` reject undefined transitions immediately instead of falling back to the same event from another state. That keeps invalid events from mutating state, appending to history, or firing callbacks.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`.

Updated the parser in [src/evaluator.py](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_017_amp-deep3_b2d_4fhy/src/evaluator.py#L80-L146) so it now handles operator precedence correctly. The fix splits parsing into `parse_primary()`, `parse_term()`, and `parse_expr()`, which makes `*` and `/` bind tighter than `+` and `-` while preserving parentheses and unary minus. I also added an EOF check so trailing junk tokens are rejected instead of being silently ignored.

Verification:
- `python -m pytest -q` -> `7 passed`
- Manual spot checks now produce the expected results, including `3 + 4 * 2 == 11` and `10 - 6 / 2 == 7`

Updated [src/conn_pool.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_018_amp-deep3_p457nvl5/src/conn_pool.py) so the pool no longer hands out dead connections.

`acquire()` now drains and discards unhealthy pooled connections before returning one, and `release()` now refuses to put closed connections back into the pool. That fixes the case where a caller closes a connection, releases it, and the next caller gets a broken handle.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `6 passed in 0.02s`.

The retry state leak is fixed. In [src/retry.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_019_amp-deep3_6u6bb2we/src/retry.py#L45-L71) and [the async variant](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_019_amp-deep3_6u6bb2we/src/retry.py#L97-L116), `attempts_used` now lives inside each wrapper call instead of the decorator closure, so every invocation gets a fresh retry budget.

I also added an async regression test in [tests/test_retry.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_019_amp-deep3_6u6bb2we/tests/test_retry.py#L120-L140) because `retry_async` had the same bug pattern as the synchronous decorator.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `7 passed`.

Fixed the DFS cycle-detection bug in [src/topo_sort.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_020_amp-deep3_tftmqacx/src/topo_sort.py#L11-L52) by adding a `visiting` recursion-stack set alongside `visited`. `topological_sort()` now raises `CyclicGraphError` for back-edges and self-loops instead of silently returning an invalid order.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `7 passed`.

Fixed the red-black delete fixup bug in [`src/rbtree.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_021_amp-deep3_m06t9jfn/src/rbtree.py#L184-L216). In both symmetric branches of `_delete_fixup`, the “sibling has two black children” case now correctly promotes the double-black state to the parent (`x = x.parent`) instead of moving to one of the parent’s child pointers, which was breaking rebalancing and causing invalid trees after some deletions.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `8 passed`, and a direct deletion/reachability check also confirmed the remaining nodes stay reachable and the tree stays valid.

Fixed the A* issue in [src/astar.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_022_amp-deep3_z7buq5tp/src/astar.py#L67-L166). The search now uses a reverse cost map from the goal to get an exact remaining-cost estimate, and the open heap still breaks ties by preferring higher `g` values in [the main search loop](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_022_amp-deep3_z7buq5tp/src/astar.py#L101-L146). I also removed the old heap behavior that relied on insertion order and had previously duplicated heap work.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`.

Updated the join resolution in [src/query_builder.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_023_amp-deep3_po0cgkzk/src/query_builder.py#L68-L79) so every implicit `JOIN ... ON ...` continues to use the base `FROM` table alias on the left-hand side, instead of switching to the most recently joined table. That fixes the incorrect SQL for 3+ table joins.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `7 passed`, confirming the multi-join cases now generate the expected `ON` clauses.

Fixed the dependency resolution bug in [src/plugin_loader.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_024_amp-deep3_yyamrlqo/src/plugin_loader.py#L86-L110). `resolve_order()` now builds the graph in the correct direction, from each dependency to the plugins that depend on it, and increments the dependent plugin’s in-degree instead of the dependency’s. That makes the topological sort return dependencies before dependents, so `load_all()` initializes plugins in the right sequence.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `7 passed in 0.02s`.

Fixed the covariance bug in [src/type_validator.py](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_025_amp-deep3_051lzenn/src/type_validator.py#L120-L239) by replacing exact inner-type comparisons with recursive validation. Lists, dict entries, and sets now reuse the subtype-aware `validate()` path, so values like `Dog` correctly satisfy schemas expecting `Animal` even inside containers.

I also updated [schema compatibility checks](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_025_amp-deep3_051lzenn/src/type_validator.py#L212-L239) to recurse through `type_params` instead of requiring exact parameter matches, which fixes cases like `List[Dog]` being accepted where `List[Animal]` is expected.

Verified with `python -m pytest -q` and `python -m py_compile src\\type_validator.py` — all tests pass.

Fixed the race in [`src/concurrent_lru.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_026_amp-deep3_27ks22o9/src/concurrent_lru.py#L43-L56) by keeping `entry.touch()`, `move_to_end()`, and the value return inside the existing `with self._lock:` block. That makes `get()` fully atomic and prevents the `OrderedDict` reordering from happening after the lock is released.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `7 passed in 0.02s`.

Fixed the nested-scope rename bug in [src/ast_rename.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_027_amp-deep3_5b2m_bsa/src/ast_rename.py). The renamer now stops descending into nested `def`, `async def`, and `lambda` scopes once it enters the target function, so shadowed locals inside inner scopes are left unchanged while the selected outer function still gets renamed correctly.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed in 0.03s`.

Fixed the error-handler ordering bug in [src/middleware.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_028_amp-deep3_tk7y94q7/src/middleware.py#L62-L65) by changing `Pipeline.handle_error()` to append handlers instead of inserting them at the front. That preserves FIFO registration order, so the first-added error handler now runs first as expected.

Verification passed with `python -m pytest -q` and the targeted fail-to-pass coverage in `tests/test_module.py`; the suite is now `6 passed`.

Fixed the infinite-loop bug in [`src/regex_engine.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_029_amp-deep3_vgu432om/src/regex_engine.py#L198-L213) by changing `+` repetition to stop once its child matches zero characters at the current position. That still allows a single empty match when the pattern requires it, but prevents the matcher from retrying the same position forever inside nested quantifiers like `(a*)+` or `([ab]*)+`.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes cleanly with `6 passed`, including the previously failing timeout cases.

Fixed the B-tree split bug in [src/btree.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_030_amp-deep3_teqgy5p0/src/btree.py#L84-L95) by changing the right-child slice from `child.keys[mid:]` to `child.keys[mid + 1:]`. That keeps the promoted median key out of the right child, which removes the duplicate-key corruption during node splits.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`, and a direct insert/reproduction run now produces a valid tree with the expected in-order result `[1, 2, ..., 10]`.

Fixed the consistent-hash ring wrap-around bug in [src/consistent_hash.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_031_amp-deep3_k1j2rwot/src/consistent_hash.py#L59-L69) by changing the `bisect_right()` overflow case to wrap to index `0` instead of the last ring entry. That makes keys hashed beyond the highest virtual node correctly map to the first node clockwise on the ring, including the exact-max-hash case.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `6 passed`.

Updated [src/template_engine.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_032_amp-deep3_wb0yttlf/src/template_engine.py#L171-L181) so each `{% for %}` iteration renders with a shallow copy of the current context instead of mutating the shared dictionary in place. That gives loop variables proper scope, prevents nested loops from leaking inner values outward, and preserves any preexisting outer variable after the loop finishes.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`.

Fixed the truncation bug in [src/varint.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_033_amp-deep3_rdg_gu49/src/varint.py) by removing the incorrect 4-iteration cap in `encode_varint()`. The encoder now keeps emitting 7-bit chunks until the entire value has been serialized, which restores correct behavior for large 32-bit and 64-bit integers.

Verification passed with `python -m pytest -q` (`7 passed`), and I also spot-checked round trips for `2**31 - 1`, `2**32`, `2**63 - 1`, and `2**64 - 1`, all of which now decode back to their original values.

Fixed the heap construction bug in [`src/merge_heap.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_034_amp-deep3_w8vkh228/src/merge_heap.py#L135-L140) by changing `_heapify()` to run the standard bottom-up heapify loop from the last non-leaf node down to the root, instead of only calling `_sift_down(0)`.

Verification:
- `python -m pytest -q` → `6 passed`
- Spot-checked merge behavior: merged heaps now validate correctly and extract in sorted order.

Updated [src/segment_tree.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_035_amp-deep3_ruoqtmc1/src/segment_tree.py#L74-L81) so `SegmentTree._range_query` now calls `_push_down(...)` before recursing on partial-overlap queries. That ensures pending lazy updates are propagated to child nodes before their sums are read, which fixes the stale-value bug described in the task.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`.

Updated the operator matcher in [src/lexer.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_036_amp-deep3_n_r5g1ay/src/lexer.py#L300-L313) to use longest-match-first tokenization. It now checks three-character operators before two-character and single-character ones, so `>>=`, `<<=`, and `...` are emitted as single tokens instead of being split apart.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`. The only remaining output is the pre-existing pytest marker warnings in the tests.

Fixed the frame-corruption bug in [`src/vm.py`](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_037_amp-deep3_77iner3_/src/vm.py#L171-L181) by restoring `BP` from the saved `CallFrame` during `RET`, alongside the existing `SP` and `IP` restoration. That keeps caller-local `LOAD`/`STORE` operations anchored to the correct frame after a function returns.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `8 passed`. The only remaining output is the pre-existing `pytest` marker warning for `pass_to_pass`, which doesn’t affect correctness.

Implemented the SERIALIZABLE phantom-read fix in [src/transaction.py](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_038_amp-deep3_ot95fs_s/src/transaction.py#L36-L273). `SERIALIZABLE` now reads from the transaction’s start-time snapshot instead of the latest committed state ([snapshot visibility](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_038_amp-deep3_ot95fs_s/src/transaction.py#L90-L122)), and serializable scans now record their scanned ranges so commit can detect concurrent writes that would have created phantoms ([range tracking and commit conflict checks](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_038_amp-deep3_ot95fs_s/src/transaction.py#L124-L250)). That covers both phantom inserts and concurrent deletes/updates affecting scanned predicates.

I also registered the existing `pass_to_pass` pytest marker in [tests/conftest.py](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_038_amp-deep3_ot95fs_s/tests/conftest.py#L3-L5) so the suite runs cleanly.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` -> `7 passed`.

Fixed the softmax-cross-entropy gradient bug in [src/neural_net.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_039_amp-deep3_sdxyv6ji/src/neural_net.py#L96-L107) by changing the backward pass to subtract `1` only at the true target index, matching `softmax(z) - one_hot(y)`.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`. Pytest still emits 3 warnings about unregistered custom marks in the test file, but they don’t affect correctness for this fix.

Fixed the tracing bug in [src/gc.py](file:///C:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_040_amp-deep3_rcr3uctx/src/gc.py#L150-L160) by making `GarbageCollector._mark()` recursively visit every referenced object via `get_all_references()`. That gives the mark phase full transitive closure from the roots, so nested lists, object fields, deep chains, and reachable cycles all survive sweep correctly.

Verified with `python -m pytest -q`: `7 passed`. Fixed the matrix math defects in [src/simd_matrix.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_074_amp-deep3_e4c41lux/src/simd_matrix.hpp#L27-L50) and corrected the transpose expectation in the local driver at [src/main.cpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_074_amp-deep3_e4c41lux/src/main.cpp#L46-L50).

The actual code changes were:
- In [Mat4::multiply](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_074_amp-deep3_e4c41lux/src/simd_matrix.hpp#L27-L50), fixed the third unrolled multiply term to read `other.at(k + 2, j)` instead of the swapped indices, which was corrupting general 4x4 multiplication results.
- In [Mat4::minor3](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_074_amp-deep3_e4c41lux/src/simd_matrix.hpp#L88-L101), fixed the final term in the 3x3 determinant expansion from `+ sub[4] * sub[6]` to `- sub[4] * sub[6]`, restoring the correct determinant calculation.
- In [test_transpose](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_074_amp-deep3_e4c41lux/src/main.cpp#L46-L50), corrected the expected transposed value from `16` to `15`.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `7 passed`, and the rebuilt binary reports `PASS` for every bundled test case.

Fixed the HAMT persistence bug in [src/hamtrie.hpp](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_075_amp-deep3_45ck1koy/src/hamtrie.hpp).

The main fix is that inserts and removes now do real copy-on-write instead of mutating shared nodes in place, so older trie versions remain unchanged. I also corrected the compressed child indexing by fixing `popcount`, and made branch creation/removal update the bitmap and child array consistently in the recursive paths at [src/hamtrie.hpp#L109-L179](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_075_amp-deep3_45ck1koy/src/hamtrie.hpp#L109-L179) and [src/hamtrie.hpp#L199-L226](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_075_amp-deep3_45ck1koy/src/hamtrie.hpp#L199-L226). I also moved the “did this key already exist?” check ahead of mutation so `size()` stays correct across persistent updates at [src/hamtrie.hpp#L66-L88](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_075_amp-deep3_45ck1koy/src/hamtrie.hpp#L66-L88).

Because the local MinGW toolchain is older, I added a small compatibility layer for `optional` in [src/hamtrie.hpp#L1-L36](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_075_amp-deep3_45ck1koy/src/hamtrie.hpp#L1-L36), so the code still exposes `.has_value()` / `.value()` and compiles in this environment.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `7 passed`.
